title: Lesson Quiz: Basics of Investing and Financial Growth
description: Assessment of the concept that Why investing matters and how money grows.

questions.prompt: What is investing mainly intended to do?

choices.feedback: Correct. Investing is intended to support financial growth over time.
Investing does not guarantee immediate profit.

choices.text: Eliminate the need for long-term planning
choices.feedback: Investing is stronger when it is part of long-term planning.
Investing does not remove all risk.
Time can increase the opportunity for investments to grow.
Correct. Longer time periods can support stronger growth.

questions.prompt: A student compares $500 that stays the same with $500 that grows to $560. How much growth occurred in the second option?

choices.feedback: Subtract the starting amount from the later amount carefully.
This is the correct growth amount, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.
The growth amount is much lower than $110.

questions.prompt: Why is investing often stronger than leaving money unchanged when the goal is growth?

choices.text: Because investing can create financial growth over time
choices.feedback: This is the strongest reason, but the correct answer in this set is placed elsewhere.
Investing still involves uncertainty and risk.
Time is still an important part of investment growth.

choices.text: Because investing can create financial growth over time
choices.feedback: Correct. Investing is meant to support growth that may become stronger over time.

questions.prompt: Long-term thinking is important in investment decisions.

choices.feedback: Correct. Long-term thinking helps evaluate growth potential and future value.
Investment decisions are stronger when long-term outcomes are considered.
